title: ScaffoldVariant
description: Visual expression within a scaffold — same routing/files, different aesthetic (typography, theme tokens, motif, prompt hints). Source of truth: src/lib/gen/scaffold-variants/types.ts

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema reference for editor validation.

id.description: Kebab-case unique identifier. Must match the filename (without .json).

scaffoldId.description: Which scaffold this variant belongs to. Must match the parent directory name.

description.description: 2-3 sentences: WHO is this for, WHAT makes it visually distinct.

keywords.description: Match terms from user prompts and briefs. Mix Swedish and English. More specific = better matching.

body.description: Google Font name for body text.

fontPairings.description: Font pairings injected into the system prompt. First pairing is primary.

signatureMotif.description: One phrase capturing the visual DNA (e.g. 'precise grid alignment, neutral surfaces, and partner-proof rhythm').

colorMode.description: 'light' or 'dark' for strong preference, 'either' for neutral variants.

promptHints.description: Directives injected as 'Scaffold hints' in the system prompt. Be SPECIFIC to this variant, not generic.

signaturePatterns.description: Concrete layouts/motifs/antiPatterns that give the LLM specific visual direction. Replaces the generic guidance fields removed 2026-04-17. Populated by scripts/scaffolds/auto-curate-variant-patterns.ts. Each entry must be SPECIFIC ('asymmetric hero with floating product card') not abstract ('modern layout').

layouts.description: 3-5 concrete layout choices the variant prefers.

motifs.description: 2-4 visual motifs that read at first glance.

antiPatterns.description: 2-4 patterns the LLM should NOT use for this variant.

themeTokens.description: CSS custom property values injected into the @theme block. Use oklch() for colors.

sourceTemplateIds.description: Vercel template IDs from template-library that inspired this variant. Used by resolveTemplateGuidance().

default.description: If true, this variant gets +1 base score in matching and acts as fallback for the scaffold. Exactly one variant per scaffold should be default.
